name: Pharmacy
description: Outpatient prescriptions, inpatient meds, drug file, formulary

items.name: PSOERXA0 DRGMTCH
items.description: This RPC is used to match incoming eRx drugs/supply items to the VistA  equivalent drug/supply items.   Input:     Both parameters are 'optional', but to receive any legitimate    results, at least one of the two must be passed in.     NDCUPN - NDC or UPN value to be match to the NDC/UPN file (#50.6

items.name: PSOERXA0 PRVMTCH
items.description: Provides logic for matching a provider identified for an incoming eRx  prescription.

items.name: PSOERXA1 INCERX
items.description: This RPC receives and processes an incoming ERX XML message, provider  check information, and patient check information.

items.name: PSOERXI1 INCERX
items.description: This RPC receives incoming eRx messages that are in the 2017 script  format. This RPC replaces PSOERXA1 INCERX, which was the RPC used for the  10.6 script format.

items.name: PSO VCC REFILL
items.description: The RPC performs a refill on an outpatient pharmacy order request.   In addition, the RPC will provide the ability in Outpatient Pharmacy  to store the source of a refill request (eg. VCC, Computerized Patient  Record System (CPRS), Outpatient Pharmacy) as well as the person  making the request - if

items.name: PSO EPCS EDIT
items.description: This remote procedure stores information on editing changes in file 200 related to the electronic prescribing of controlled substances.

items.name: PSO EPCS ADD DEA
items.description: INPUT:  DATA - DEA DATA LINE "^" DELIMITED.         NPIEN - IEN OF PROVIDER TO BE LINKED TO THIS DEA NUMBER OUTPUT: RET - DEA IEN for SUCCESS, 0 for UNSUCCESSFUL

items.name: PSO EPCS DEADOJ
items.description: This RPC call accepts a DEA Number as input. It calls the DOJ/DEA Web Service to get the most recent information for the provider which is  returned to the calling program in a single string with "^" delimited data. The values in the string are:     1 - PROVIDER NAME   2 - ADDRESS 1   3 - ADDRESS 2

items.name: PSO EPCS MBM
items.description: This RPC is provided to ePCS GUI to check if the site is setup for Meds by Mail service.

items.name: PSO EPCS REMOVE DEA
items.description: Functionality to remove a DEA multiple from file #200, Field 53.21   INPUT:  NPIEN - NEW PERSON FILE #200 INTERNAL ENTRY NUMBER           DEATXT - PROPERLY FORMATTED DEA NUMBER   OUTPUT: RET - 1 for SUCCESS, 0 for UNSUCCESSFUL

items.name: PSO EPCS DEA DUP CHECK
items.description: This RPC will accept a DEA in text format, and an institutional suffix if  available.  It will perform checking to determine if the DEA is being  used by another user.

items.name: PSO EPCS DEALIST
items.description: This remote procedure call will provide a list of active DEA #s for a  given provider.   INPUT:  NPIEN - NEW PERSON FILE #200 INTERNAL ENTRY NUMBER   OUTPUT: RET - A STRING OF DEA INFORMATION DELIMITED BY THE "^"           1 - DEA NUMBER           2 - INDIVIDUAL DEA SUFFIX           3 - STATE

items.name: PSO EPCS VERSION
items.description: This RPC is used to check the version of the ePCS GUI that should be  running.

items.name: PSO EPCS DETOX CHECK
items.description: This RPC will accept a DEA number in text format, and a DETOX number in  text format.  It will perform checking to see if the DETOX number is a  valid format, and will checking if the DETOX number is being used by  another DEA number.

items.name: PSO EPCS VA# DUP CHECK
items.description: This RPC will accept a VA# in text format, and a provider's NEW PERSON File #200 IEN (Internal Entry number).  It will perform checking  to determine if the VA# is being used by another provider.

items.name: PSO EPCS FILER
items.description: This RPC will accept a Field Number in the New Person, File #200, a single data item in text format, and Provider Internal Entry Number (IEN) in  File #200.

items.name: PSO EPCS REPORTS
items.description: This call is used by all ePrescribing Controlled Substances (ePCS) GUI  reports. It produces a report based on the criteria selected through the  ePCS GUI application.

items.name: PSO EPCS SYSTEM DATE TIME
items.description: Broker call returns the server date as a FileMan internal and external  date format.

items.name: PSO EPCS FIELD HELP
items.description: Get the field help from the data dictionary based on the field number.

items.name: PSO EPCS GET LIST
items.description: This call is used to perform a search on a file based on a search string.

items.name: PSO EPCS LIST NP SCHED
items.description: Functionality to list the New Person file #200 Schedules for a provider. Schedule fields are 55.1,55.2,55.3,55.4,55.5,55.6

items.name: PSO EPCS FILE NP SCHED
items.description: Functionality to file the New Person file #200 Schedules for a provider. Schedule fields are 55.1,55.2,55.3,55.4,55.5,55.6

items.name: PSO EPCS LIST OPTN DESC
items.description: RPC to return Option file #19 description word processing text.

items.name: PSO EPCS TOPIC HELP
items.description: Returns the text from the HELP FRAME file (#9.2) based on a help frame.

items.name: PSO ACTIVITY LOG
items.description: This RPC allows retrieval of the Activity Log for a specific prescription  number (External) in the PRESCRIPTION (#52) file.   Return list of ACTIVITY transactions for a particular PRESCRIPTION  (#52)  file entry.  Each transaction will have the following fields (if populated in VistA):      activit

items.name: PSO CMOP LOG
items.description: This RPC allows retrieval of the CMOP Log for a specific prescription  number   Each transaction will have the following fields:            dateShipped         #52.01    9          ndcReceived         #52.01    4          rxReference         #52.01    2          sequenceNumber      #52.01    1

items.name: PSO COPAY LOG
items.description: Return values from Copay Transaction log in PRESCRIPTION (#52) file.   Possible Return Values:   RESULTS(0) = -n^Error message   RESULTS(0) = 0^No Data   RESULTS(1) = Array of return values in JSON format:     copayActivityLog          #52.0107, .01   reason                    #52.0107, 1   initiato

items.name: PSO ECME LOG
items.description: Return list of ECME transactions for a particular PRESCRIPTION (#52) file entry. Each transaction will have the following fields:   ecmeLogDate          #52.3, .01   reason               #52.3, .02    initiatorOfActivity  #52.3, .03   rxReference          #52.3, .04   comment              #52.3, .05

items.name: PSO ERX LOG
items.description: Return list of ERX transactions for a particular PRESCRIPTION (#52) file entry. Each transaction will have the following fields:   eRxLogDate           #52.3, .01   reason               #52.3, .02    initiatorOfActivity  #52.3, .03   rxReference          #52.3, .04   comment              #52.3, .05

items.name: PSO LABEL LOG
items.description: Allow listing of Label Log (#52.032) subfile in the PRESCRIPTION (#52) file, given an external prescription number.   RETURN VARIABLES:           0^No data - there are no LABEL entries for this prescription"           -1^ Prescription Number is Required"         -2^ Prescription Number is not recogn

items.name: PSO LOT EXPIRATION
items.description: Return list of LOT/EXP transactions for a particular PRESCRIPTION (#52)) file entry. Each Transaction will have the following fields:   expirationDate   #52.0401, 1   lotExp           #52.0401, .01   rxIndicator      #52.0401, 2

items.name: PSO PARTIALS LOG
items.description: This RPC allows retrieval of the Partials Log for a specific prescription. Each transaction will have the following fields (if populated in VistA):                                                 loginDateTime                  #52.2, .08    partialDate                    #52.2, .01     qty

items.name: PSO REFILL LOG
items.description: This RPC allows retrieval of the Refill Log for a specific prescription  number.   Return list of REFILL transactions for a particular PRESCRIPTION  (#52) file entry.   Each transaction will have the following fields (if populated in VistA):      loginDate                            #52.1, 7    refi

items.name: PSO EPCS CRED
items.description: Return the last CREDENTIAL TYPE from SIGN-ON LOG file (#3.081) for user DUZ passed in.

items.name: PSO EPCS PSDRPH FILER
items.description: Allocates and deallocates the PSDRPH key to the specified user and logs  the event in the XUEPCS PSDRPH AUDIT (#8991.7) file.

items.name: PSO ADDRESS UPDATE
items.description: This RPC allows the address fields in the PATIENT (#2) file to be updated. It takes two parameters: the patient ICN and the temporary address fields that are to be updated.

items.name: PSO GET ADDRESS
items.description: get a patient address

items.name: PSO TEMP ADDRESS ACTIVE FLAG
items.description: This RPC allows the setting of the TEMPORARY ADDRESS ACTIVE? field in  the patient file (field .12105 in file #2)

items.name: PSO DEL TEMPORARY ADDRESS
items.description: This RPC deletes the patient's temporary address from the patient file

items.name: PSB SCANPT
items.description: This RPC is used to validate the data scanned in at the scan patient  wristband prompt of the mnOpenPatient component.  The value passed in  is either the full SSN scanned in from the patient wristband -or- the  1U4N syntax of the patient lookup.  In either case the call must  return only one patien

items.name: PSB INSTRUCTOR
items.description: Used by frmInstructor to validate that an instructor is at the client  with a student.  Validation is acheived via the instructor entering  their SSN and electronic signature code.  This is then validated  against the NEW PERSON file (#200).  If a valid user is obtained,  that user must posses the P

items.name: PSB USERLOAD
items.description: This RPC is called at application startup to populate the BCMA_User  object with the users defaults.  No parameters are passed, the current  DUZ is assumed.

items.name: PSB USERSAVE
items.description: Save the users current window settings for the next session.

items.name: PSB FMDATE
items.description: Used to validate Fileman dates.

items.name: PSB SCANMED
items.description: Takes the scanned valued from the medication and does a lookup on file  50 for an exact match.  If more than one or less than one entry are  found for the lookup an error is returned to the client.

items.name: PSB PARAMETER
items.description: Called by client to return or set parameters

items.name: PSB TRANSACTION
items.description: This is the filing RPC for all data returning from the client regarding the medication log.  Filing is handled by business rules on the server and this RPC will return either '1^Data Filed' or '-1^reason for not filing data' to the client.  Results of the  processed transaction is communicated via t

items.name: PSB SERVER CLOCK VARIANCE
items.description: Client date/time in external FileMan format. Returns the variance from the server to the client in minutes.

items.name: PSB MEDICATION HISTORY
items.description: Returns the history of a medication for a patient from the orderable item.

items.name: PSB GETPRNS
items.description: Returns all administrations of a PRN order that have NOT had the PRN Effectiveness documented in the current admission or within the hours  defined in PRN documentation site parameter whichever is greater of the  two.  When the PRN medication is administered a flag is set based on the  given PRN Rea

items.name: PSB REPORT
items.description: Return the text for the specified report and the user has the option to  print the reports.

items.name: PSB SUBMIT MISSING DOSE
items.description: Allows the client to submit a missing dose interactively.

items.name: PSB VALIDATE ESIG
items.description: Validate the data in PSBESIG against the user currently signed on (DUZ)

items.name: PSB LOCK
items.description: LOCKING NO LONGER USED AS OF PATCH 7.  INCLUDED FOR BACKWARD COMPATABILITY WITH GUI.  CALL WILL BE REMOVED IN PATCH 8.

items.name: PSB CHECK SERVER
items.description: Returns -1 or 1 when checking for patches and build on the server.

items.name: PSB MAIL
items.description: A call is made that allows the GUI to send its' own formatted mail message.

items.name: PSB GETORDERTAB
items.description: Gives the client VDL information for the specified patient and time frame.

items.name: PSB WARDLIST
items.description: Returns a list of active wards that are available for the definition of IV parameters in the BCMA IV PARAMETERS file 53.66. Wards already in file 53.66 are returned with the type of IV PARAMETERS defined.

items.name: PSB GETIVPAR
items.description: Returns the IV parameters by IV type, as defined for a ward in file 53.66.

items.name: PSB DEVICE
items.description: Allows user to select a Printer from the GUI.

items.name: PSB PUTIVPAR
items.description: Sets 53.66 or parameters file w/input from 53.66 if ward is not "ALL"

items.name: PSB IV ORDER HISTORY
items.description: Returns individual detailed bag history.

items.name: PSB BAG DETAIL
items.description: Returns a chronological detailed history on each specific IV bag that is selected.     [0] = -1^No History On File                [0] = Number of Nodes [1] = Action Date/Time^User ID^Action Status^Comments

items.name: PSB ALLERGY
items.description: Returns a list of allergies for a patient.

items.name: PSB GETPROVIDER
items.description: Used to get a list of active providers.

items.name: PSB MOB DRUG LIST
items.description: Used by the BCMA/CPRS Med Order Button to return an array of drug.

items.name: PSB CPRS ORDER
items.description: This RPC sets results of a Med Order Button transaction in a global for  Inpatient Pharmacy to pick up.

items.name: PSB NURS WARDLIST
items.description: This will return a list of active ward from the NURS LOCATION, file 211.4.

items.name: PSB MAXDAYS
items.description: This RPC returns the maximum number of days a user can view or print the  MAH report. This parameter is set using CPRS and is call ORRP BCMA MAH.

items.name: PSB VERSION CHECK
items.description: This RPC is called at startup. No parameters are passed.

items.name: PSB CHECK IV
items.description: RPC PSB CHECK IV - When given a patient's data file number(DFN), this  process will return infusing IV information pertaining to the given  DFN.  The information will be returned at the location presented by the "RESULTS" parameter.

items.name: PSB VITALS
items.description: Passes array of Vital entries - Temp,Pulse,Resp,BP,Pain in the last 7 days

items.name: PSB VITAL MEAS FILE
items.description: This RPC has been built specifically to process the filing of BCMA  Pain Score data. The processing of other VITAL type may be incorporated  with some adjustments.       This routine is to service BCMA 3.0 functionality and store VITALs'   data into the VITAL MEASUREMENT FILE - ^GMR(120.5  using the

items.name: PSB MED LOG LOOKUP
items.description: BCMA Medication Log Look Up Remote Procedures.   This routine is a conglomerate of  Medication Log lookup functionality per the BCMA Graphical User Interface software.   Input:  PSBREC (array)         PSBREC (0)     determine  "lookup" function                        "PTLKUP" (patient file (#2) look

items.name: PSB COVERSHEET1
items.description: (modified 05/08/2007)   PSB COVERSHEET1 is to return order data per patient and construction of  BCMA Coversheet view as presented by the BCMA-HSC Coversheet Phase II SRS and SDD documentation.   INPUT:  communications area -  ""          patient's DFN       -  DFN     (patient ptr.)

items.name: PSB UTL XSTATUS SRCH
items.description: RESULTS(0)=returned line count RESULTS(1)=patients location during activity RESULTS(2)=medication^ordernumber RESULTS(3)= action fileman date&time RESULTS(4)= scheduled administration fileman date&time

items.name: PSB MAN SCAN FAILURE
items.description: SCANFAIL(RESULTS,PSBPARAM)      ;  TEJ 05/12/2006  BCMA-Managing Scanning Failures (MSF)         ;       Process Scanning Failures         ;               Parameters:         ;               Input (via GUI):         ;        Per Wristband  (0)      -       Pat IEN ^ ^ Failure Reason ^ User's Comment

items.name: PSB GETINJECTIONSITE
items.description: Get the last nn injections site info from the BCMA MEDICATION LOG file  #53.9.   Get only of specific Orderable Items per patient in reverse chronology date/time order or All Orderable Items per patient in  reverse chronology date/time order.

items.name: PSB WITNESS
items.description: Used by frmWintess to validate if a Witness at the client is authorized  to be a witness for a BCMA action, i.e. High Risk Drug administration.   Validation is achieved via not allowing the logged in person to witness  for themselves, also persons that hold certain keys can't be a valid  witness.

items.name: PSB CLINICLIST
items.description: Returns Active Clinic names based on Search criteria.

items.name: PSB MEDS ON PATIENT
items.description: This RPC returns indicators if medications are still on a patient.  The  patient's DFN will be passed in and a RESULTS array returned with three  flags set.  Flags set to 1 for meds are on patient or 0 none per this category.

items.name: PSB GETSETWP
items.description: This RPC allows the GUI to add and update body site locations graphically  by sending the coordinates of the loction on the body and storing these using word processing free form text type field.

items.name: PSN FDA MED GUIDE PRINT QUEUE
items.description: This Remote Proceedure Call is used by a Java Client to print FDA Medication Guides for the National Drug File (NDF) application.

items.name: PSA UPLOAD
items.description: This is the primary entry point for the Drug Accountability Upload GUI.

items.rpcName: PSN FDA MED GUIDE PRINT QUEUE
